title: Eugenia barbosae
taxonomy: Eugenia barbosae belongs to the family Myrtaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family includes over 3,000 species, many of which are native to the tropics and subtropics. Eugenia barbosae is a genus of flowering plants, with Eugenia barbosae being a specific species within this genus. The Myrtaceae family includes a wide range of plants, from small shrubs to large trees, and many species are cultivated for their ornamental value or edible fruits. Eugenia barbosae is a member of the order Myrtales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Melastomataceae and Alzateaceae.
